action.reasoning: Observation: v4 heuristic max safe_steps=191 (avg~99 in test, ~53 in 50-ep eval), never reaches 500; config revealed exact success_angle_dist=0.2618 (~15deg), facing_threshold=0.5, need_to_face_human=True, must_call_stop=True, max_steps=750; one epi reached 191 but avg_dist still ~4-5m. Analysis: proportional control loses dynamic random-walking target (oracle changes direction); gains caused oscillation or insufficient catch-up speed; manual safe count now must use 0.2618 not pi/6; SPF heuristic from literature (~0.5 follow-rate) is promising but complex (needs world human pos from localization+other_agent_gps + discrete-to-vel mapping). Root cause: heuristic too weak for persistent following over 500 steps. Decision: implement v5 heuristic (stronger linear=clip(dist-1.0,-0.6,1.0), angular=-4.5*angle, pure-rotate on |angle|>0.6, slow stable follow when in-range+detector, backup on <0.9m); extract exact success_angle_dist from config for safe counting; expand test to 5 episodes + more diagnostics (collision rate, per-epi safe ratio); rewrite full non-truncated predict.py with identical v5 + complete JSON contract; run test first to validate improvement before submit_eval.
